prompt.content: Use Python to analyze the potential impact of market consolidation to StreamVibe. The content_catalog.csv provides key attributes of the current catalog of contents, including the content availability. The content_metadata_extended.csv contains additional metadata information about the catalog of contents, including licensing details. The content_impressions.csv details homepage/search impressions and clicks. The subscribers.csv lists the accounts, user demographics and key account information. For this analysis, treat the terms of account, user and subscriber as interchangeable. The user_preferences_survey.csv records user survey responses, including feature requests. Netflix and Warner are in active mergers and acquisitions (M&A) discussion. If combined, the new company (Netflix-Warner) would own two big content producers - Netflix Productions and Warner Bros. Identify contents owned by Netflix-Warner with licenses expiring before (<) 2027. Analyze the downstream impact if the new company decides to pull these contents from StreamVibe. Report the number of contents and the percentage (rounded to one decimal point) of total currently available contents that StreamVibe will lose. For the movie and tv_show content types separately, report the percentage (rounded to one decimal point) of total currently available content duration lost. Define users at risk of churn as those who clicked at least two different titles within the affected content set or who ever requested the feature of "More genres". Report the number of currently active (account status) users StreamVibe may lose as a result. Provide python code along with the results.

rubrics.name: Reports the number of contents that StreamVibe will lose as exactly 474 (ver_250f67156f104298b36dea174a20ec3a)

messages.content: Reports the number of contents that StreamVibe will lose as exactly 474.

rubrics.name: Reports the percentage of total currently available contents that StreamVibe... (ver_655e670396194c6da48f9858e62096e0)

messages.content: Reports the percentage of total currently available contents that StreamVibe will lose as 10.0% +/- 0.2.

rubrics.name: Reports the percentage of total currently available movie content duration... (ver_15250e05aa5343bfb362db5f59b9d56f)

messages.content: Reports the percentage of total currently available movie content duration lost as 17.3% +/- 0.2.

rubrics.name: Reports the percentage of total currently available tv show content duration... (ver_8e4180c2d6a7479e9fa0bed51301a362)

messages.content: Reports the percentage of total currently available tv show content duration lost as 15.1% +/- 0.2.

rubrics.name: Reports the number of currently active (account status) users StreamVibe may... (ver_51fb7c711c4a425da38df084201e88bc)

messages.content: Reports the number of currently active (account status) users StreamVibe may lose as exactly 237.

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
the number of contents that StreamVibe will lose: 474
the percentage (rounded to one decimal point) of total currently available contents that StreamVibe will lose: 10.0%
For movie, the percentage (rounded to one decimal point) of total currently available content duration lost: 17.3%
For tv_show, the percentage (rounded to one decimal point) of total currently available content duration lost: 15.1%
the number of currently active (account status) users StreamVibe may lose: 237

```
import pandas as pd


df_catalog = pd.read_csv('./csv_datasets/content_catalog.csv')
df_metadata = pd.read_csv('./csv_datasets/content_metadata_extended.csv')
df_impressions = pd.read_csv('./csv_datasets/content_impressions.csv')
df_subscribers = pd.read_csv('./csv_datasets/subscribers.csv')
df_survey = pd.read_csv('./csv_datasets/user_preferences_survey.csv')


# filter for currently available contents
df_catalog_available = df_catalog[df_catalog.is_available == 1]


# Identify contents owned by Netflix Productions and Warner Bros and expiring before 2027
df_catalogue_meta = df_catalog_available.merge(df_metadata, on="content_id", how="left")
df_lostContent = df_catalogue_meta.loc[
    (df_catalogue_meta.content_producer.isin(["Netflix Productions", "Warner Bros"]))
    & (pd.to_datetime(df_catalogue_meta.license_expiry_date).dt.year < 2027)
]


print(f"the number of contents that StreamVibe will lose: {df_lostContent.shape[0]}")
print(
    f"the percentage (rounded to one decimal point) of total currently available contents that StreamVibe will lose: {df_lostContent.shape[0]/df_catalog_available.shape[0]:.1%}"
)


# calcualte the percentage of content duration lost for "movie" and "tv_show", spearately
for content_type in ("movie", "tv_show"):
    ttl_duration = (
        df_catalog_available.loc[df_catalog_available.content_type == content_type]
        .apply(
            lambda s: (
                s["content_duration_minutes"]
                if pd.isnull(s["total_episodes"])
                else s["content_duration_minutes"] * s["total_episodes"]
            ),
            axis=1,
        )
        .sum()
    )

    lost_duration = (
        df_lostContent.loc[df_lostContent.content_type == content_type]
        .apply(
            lambda s: (
                s["content_duration_minutes"]
                if pd.isnull(s["total_episodes"])
                else s["content_duration_minutes"] * s["total_episodes"]
            ),
            axis=1,
        )
        .sum()
    )

    print(
        f"For {content_type}, the percentage (rounded to one decimal point) of total currently available content duration lost: {lost_duration/ttl_duration:.1%}"
    )


# identify at risk users

# part 1, users clicked at least two lost contents
lostContent_id = df_lostContent.content_id.unique()
df_userClickContentCnt = (
    df_impressions.loc[
        (df_impressions.content_id.isin(lostContent_id))
        & (df_impressions.clicked == True)
    ]
    .groupby("user_id")["content_id"]
    .nunique()
)
atRiskUser_1 = df_userClickContentCnt[df_userClickContentCnt > 1].index.tolist()

# part 2, users who ever requested "More genres" feature
atRiskUser_2 = (
    df_survey.loc[df_survey.feature_requests == "More genres", "user_id"]
    .unique()
    .tolist()
)

df_atRiskUser = df_subscribers.loc[
    (df_subscribers.account_status == "active")
    & (df_subscribers.user_id.isin(set(atRiskUser_1 + atRiskUser_2)))
]

print(
    f"the number of currently active (account status) users StreamVibe may lose: {df_atRiskUser.shape[0]}"
)
```
